tool_design.role_in_intervention: Case pathway for moving conflict through direct conversation, facilitation, and escalation when needed.
tool_design.usage_moment: Whenever interpersonal conflict needs a documented path
tool_design.expected_output: Conflict case log with stage, agreements, and review date

failure_warnings.warning: Entries are logged late or inconsistently.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Patterns and closure loops stay hidden.
early_signals: Cases are discussed verbally but not logged
prevention: Tie updates to a fixed review cadence

failure_warnings.warning: Tracker is updated but no action follows.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The same communication issue repeats despite being visible.

content:
# Conflict Resolution Pathway & Case Log **Tool ID:** T076-03 **Block:** B076 (Communication Culture & Feedback) **Domain:** Team & Talent (D04_team_talent) **Level:** L2 **Format:** Tracker **Role:** Manager / Owner **Time Required:** 5 minutes to classify, 10-20 minutes per stage, 2 minutes to log **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For This tool keeps conflict from drifting into gossip, avoidance, or formal grievance too early. It gives you: - a clean pathway for what happens first, second, and third - a record of what was tried - a review date so issues do not disappear and then return Use it when the issue is between people rather than a single standards conversation. --- ## The 3-Stage Pathway | Stage | Use When | Owner | Output | |---|---|---|---| | Stage 1 - Direct conversation | Two people can still speak directly and safely | The people involved | One clear agreement | | Stage 2 - Facilitated conversation | Stage 1 failed, was avoided, or needs support | Manager | Shared understanding + next steps | | Stage 3 - Formal escalation check | Safety, discrimination, harassment, retaliation, or repeated unresolved issue | Owner / formal route | Decision to escalate or close | --- ## Fast Triage Check
  • Is anyone unsafe?
  • Is there a harassment, discrimination, or bullying concern?
  • Is food safety or legal compliance involved?
  • Has this already been addressed informally more than once?
If any answer is yes, do not rely on informal resolution alone. --- ## Stage 1 - Direct Conversation Prompt Use this if both people can still engage professionally. Suggested script: - "I want to clear something early so it doesn't keep affecting the shift." - "What happened from your side?" - "The impact on the team / service was..." - "What do we need to do differently next time?" **Stage 1 agreement:** ________________________________________ **Review date:** ______________________ --- ## Stage 2 - Facilitated Conversation Structure Use this when direct conversation has failed or is being avoided. **Manager rules for the room:** - one person speaks at a time - facts before interpretation - impact before blame - one agreement before close **Agenda:** | Step | Prompt | |---|---| | 1 | What happened? | | 2 | What impact did it have on work, trust, or service? | | 3 | What needs to change immediately? | | 4 | What support or boundary is needed? | | 5 | When will we review it? | --- ## Case Log | Case ID | Date Opened | People Involved | Issue Summary | Current Stage | Owner | Review Date | Status | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | | | | | Stage 1 / Stage 2 / Stage 3 | | | Open / Closed / Escalated | | | | | | Stage 1 / Stage 2 / Stage 3 | | | Open / Closed / Escalated | | | | | | Stage 1 / Stage 2 / Stage 3 | | | Open / Closed / Escalated | --- ## Case Detail Record **Case ID:** ______________________ **Date opened:** ______________________ **Opened by:** ______________________ **People involved:** ________________________________________ **What happened:** ________________________________________ ________________________________________ **Impact on shift / team / guest experience:** ________________________________________ ________________________________________ **Stage used today:** [ ] Stage 1 direct conversation [ ] Stage 2 facilitated conversation [ ] Stage 3 escalation review **Agreement reached:** ________________________________________ ________________________________________ **Support / boundary agreed:** ________________________________________ **Review date:** ______________________ **Outcome at review:** [ ] closed [ ] monitor further [ ] escalated formally --- ## Escalate Instead If - someone reports bullying, discrimination, harassment, or retaliation - one party fears speaking directly - there is serious dishonesty or tampering with records - there is immediate safety risk - the same pair is repeating the same issue with no behavioural change --- ## Common Mistakes - Treating gossip as evidence. - Forcing a direct conversation when one person clearly feels unsafe. - Letting mediation become a debate about personality. - Closing the issue without a review date. - Saying "sorted" when nothing specific was actually agreed. --- ## Connected Resources **Used alongside:** - T076-01 - SBI Feedback Quick Card - T076-04 - Difficult Conversations Guide - T076-05 - Communication Culture Survey & Scorecard **Parent block:** B076 - Communication Culture & Feedback --- *Tool Version 2.0 - built to stop low-grade friction from becoming a culture problem.*
